{{基礎情報 公家
| 氏名 = Shiinetsuhiko
| 画像 =
| 画像サイズ = 220px
| 画像説明 =
| 時代 = Ancient
| 生誕 = Unknown
| 死没 = Unknown
| 改名 =
| 別名 = Saonetsuhiko
| 諡号 =
| 神号 =
| 戒名 =
| 墓所 =
| 官位 = {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}
| 主君 = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}}
| 氏族 = Ancestor of the {{ill|Yamato clan|ja|倭氏}}, {{ill|Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|久比岐国造}}, {{ill|Akashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|明石国造}}, etc.
| 父母 = {{ill|Takekurai-no-Mikoto|ja|武位起命}}
| 妻   =
| 子   =
| 特記事項 = Self-identified as a {{ill|kunitsukami|ja|国津神}}
}}
[[File:Shinetsuhiko.jpg|thumb|220px|Shiinetsuhiko, from ''{{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|ja|前賢故実}}'']]
'''Shiinetsuhiko''' (''shiinetsuhiko''; ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}''), '''Saonetsuhiko''' (''saonetsuhiko''; ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''), is a {{ill|kami|ja|神 (神道)|lt=kami|lt_ja=神}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. He is the ancestor of the {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}} (Yamato-no-Atai-be), or founder of the Ōyamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko, Ōyamato-no-Atai, who appears in the {{ill|Eastern Expedition of Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武東征|lt=Eastern Expedition of Emperor Jimmu|lt_ja=神武東征}}. Also Uzuhiko (''uzuhiko''; ''Nihon Shoki'') or Uzuhiko (''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録}}''). Also Kamushiritsuhiko (''kamushiritsuhiko''; ''Shinsen Shōjiroku'')<ref>{{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清}}, ''Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū: Kōshō-hen Vol. 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1982, pp. 85-95</ref><ref>Saeki Arikiyo, ''Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū: Honbun-hen'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1981, p. 251</ref>.

== Overview ==
He is a {{ill|kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} whom {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} met at {{ill|Hayasui-no-To|ja|速吸門}} during the Eastern Expedition, and he became the guide of the sea route. At this time, the ''Nihon Shoki'' states that he was fishing at Wada-no-Ura when Emperor Jimmu extended a pole of {{ill|shii|ja|シイ|lt=shii|lt_ja=椎}} wood to him and pulled him into the royal ship, changing his name from Uzuhiko to Shiinetsuhiko; the ''Kojiki'' states that he was riding on the {{ill|shell|ja|甲羅}} of a {{ill|turtle|ja|カメ|lt=turtle|lt_ja=亀}} when a pole was extended and he was pulled into the royal ship and given the name Saonetsuhiko.

In the ''Nihon Shoki'', he thereafter submitted plans to Emperor Jimmu and defeated {{ill|E-Shiki|ja|兄磯城}} by a pincer attack. After Emperor Jimmu's accession, he was appointed {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}} as a reward.

There are various views on Hayasui-no-To. In the ''Nihon Shoki'', it is thought to refer to the {{ill|Hōyo Strait|ja|豊予海峡}}, and at Saganoseki, {{ill|Ōita, Ōita|ja|大分市|lt=Ōita City|lt_ja=大分市}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|ja|大分県}}, there is a Shiinetsuhiko Shrine enshrining Shiinetsuhiko. In the ''Kojiki'', it is thought to refer to the mouth of {{ill|Kojima Bay|ja|児島湾}} in {{ill|Kibi Province|ja|吉備国}}. At Suimoncho, Higashi-ku, {{ill|Okayama (city)|ja|岡山市|lt=Okayama City|lt_ja=岡山市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}, there is the {{ill|Kameiwa Shrine|ja|亀石まつり|lt=Kameiwa Shrine|lt_ja=亀石神社}} (''kameiwa-jinja'') that enshrines Kame-iwa (turtle rock), said to be the incarnation of the great turtle on which Uzuhiko (Uzuhiko-no-Mikoto) rode. Or, from the point that the ''Kojiki'' states that they passed through Hayasui-no-To between Kibi's Takashima-no-Miya and Naniwa, there is also a view that this refers to the {{ill|Akashi Strait|ja|明石海峡}}<ref>''Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei 67: Nihon Shoki, vol. 1'', 1967, Iwanami Shoten, p. 190, top note</ref>. In the ''Nihon Shoki'' Kamiyo jō, Section 5, Issho No. 10, it says "Then went to see Awa-no-To and Hayasui-na-To; however the tides of both these two straits were already very rapid," and if "Awa-no-To" is the strait between {{ill|Awaji Province|ja|淡路国}} and {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国}}, then the Hayasui-no-To alongside it would be the Akashi Strait, and the ''Kojiki'' seems to be correct<ref>Saeki Arikiyo, ''Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū: Kōshō-hen Vol. 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1982, p. 92</ref>. According to the ''Awa-shi'', Hayasui-na-To is the {{ill|Naruto Strait|ja|鳴門海峡}}<ref>''Awa-shi'', Vol. 3</ref>.

Additionally, among the shrines whose enshrined deity is Shiinetsuhiko-no-Mikoto, there is {{ill|Hokura Shrine|ja|保久良神社}} in {{ill|Motoyama, Kobe|ja|本山町 (神戸市)|lt=Motoyama-chō|lt_ja=本山町}}, {{ill|Higashinada-ku, Kobe|ja|東灘区|lt=Higashinada-ku|lt_ja=東灘区}}, {{ill|Kobe|ja|神戸市|lt=Kobe City|lt_ja=神戸市}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}. According to the origin document of Hokura Shrine: "The reasons for the shrine name are: 1. The descendants of Shiinetsuhiko-no-Mikoto, the Kurahito-Mizumori, enshrine their ancestors, and 2. They store war trophies from the Three Koreas campaign," and it says he appeared at Hayasui-no-To (Akashi Strait) during the Eastern Expedition of Emperor Jimmu to guide the army.

There is a tradition that Shiinetsuhiko-no-Mikoto drifted ashore riding on the back of a blue turtle (''ōgi'') at the beach of {{ill|Aoki (Kobe)|ja|青木 (神戸市)|lt=Ōgi|lt_ja=青木}}, Higashinada-ku, Kobe City, south of Hokura Shrine, which became the origin of the place name Ōgi<ref>''Kobe no Jinja: Shinai 310 sha Annai'', 2000, ed. {{ill|Hyōgo Shrine Office|ja|神社庁|lt=Hyōgo Shrine Office|lt_ja=神社庁}}, Kobe City Branch</ref>.

Yoshii Yoshitaka speculates regarding Hokura Shrine: "Shiinetsuhiko-no-Mikoto is said to have been the chieftain of the Ama (sea clan) who ruled the northern shore of {{ill|Osaka Bay|ja|大阪湾}}, and that this shrine is the original shrine of the Oku-Ebisu Shrine at Nishinomiya Ebisu ({{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社}} in {{ill|Nishinomiya, Hyōgo|ja|西宮市|lt=Nishinomiya City|lt_ja=西宮市}}, Hyōgo Prefecture)"<ref>''Nihon no Kamigami — Jinja to Seichi 3: Settsu, Kawachi, Izumi, Awaji'', 2000, {{ill|Tanigawa Ken'ichi|ja|谷川健一}}, {{ill|Ōwa Iwao|ja|大和岩雄}}, {{ill|Ochiai Shigenobu|ja|落合重信}}, Kawajiri Tadashi, Kobayashi Akira, Takeda Shin'ichi, Tamura Toshihisa, Tōse Hiroshi, Hamaoka Kimiko, Hayashi Rikio, Furuta Minoru, Matsushita Hikaru, Matsumoto Suikō, eds., {{ill|Hakusuisha|ja|白水社}}</ref>.
[[ファイル:YamatoSukune.jpg|サムネイル|293x293ピクセル|Yamato-Sukune at Kono Shrine]]

== Variant Traditions ==
At {{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社}} (''kono-jinja'') on the Tango Peninsula, it says: "Alternate names: '''Uzuhiko''', '''Shiinetsuhiko''', '''Kamushiritsuhiko'''. Fourth-generation descendant of the chief enshrined deity of Kono-gū, the heavenly grandson {{ill|Hikohohoakari-no-Mikoto|ja|彦火明命}}; fourth-generation ancestor of the Kaifu-gūjike. At the {{ill|Eastern Expedition of Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武東征}}, he appeared on a turtle at {{ill|Akashi Strait|ja|明石海峡}} ({{ill|Hayasui-no-To|ja|速吸門}}), guided {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} onward to Naniwa, Kawachi, and Yamato, and, for his many plans, was given the title '''Yamato-Sukune''' (''yamato-sukune'') by Emperor Jimmu as the first contributor to the founding of Yamato. He is also called Ōyamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko and Yamato-no-Atai." And there is a statue of '''Yamato-Sukune''' riding on the back of a turtle within the precinct.

== Genealogy ==
According to the tradition of {{ill|Shiinetsuhiko Shrine|ja|椎根津彦神社}}, his father was {{ill|Takekurai-no-Mikoto|ja|武位起命}}, son of {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ|lt=Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=彦火火出見尊}}, and his younger sister was {{ill|Shōji-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|祥持姫命}}.
The "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''Kujiki'' records a variant tradition that makes Shiinetsuhiko the grandson of Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto, and the Kōson Hongi records a variant tradition that Shiinetsuhiko's grandfather was Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto and his father was Takekurai-no-Mikoto<ref>Saeki Arikiyo, ''Shinsen Shōjiroku no Kenkyū: Kōshō-hen Vol. 4'', Yoshikawa Kōbunkan, 1982, p. 91</ref>.

== Descendant Clans ==
*{{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|倭国造}}
*{{ill|Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|久比岐国造}} — During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|崇神天皇}}, Mihoko-no-Mikoto, the 5th-generation descendant of Shiinetsuhiko, was appointed.
*{{ill|Akashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|明石国造}} — During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, Tsumiji-no-Sukune, the 8th-generation descendant of Shiinetsuhiko, was appointed.
*{{ill|Aomi clan|ja|青海氏}} — A branch clan of the Kubiki-no-Atai of the Kubiki no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage.
*{{ill|Kibi-Amabe clan|ja|吉備海部氏}}
*{{ill|Yosami-Mononoimi clan|ja|依羅物忌氏}}
*{{ill|Ama clan|ja|海氏}}

== References ==
<references/>

== External links ==
* [http://www.hayasuihime.biz-web.jp/page444.html Shiinetsuhiko Shrine]

== See also ==
*{{ill|List of Figures from before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧}}
*{{ill|Hokura Shrine|ja|保久良神社}}
*{{ill|Aomi Shrine|ja|青海神社}}
*{{ill|Shiinetsuhiko Shrine|ja|椎根津彦神社}}
*{{ill|Shimazu Uzuhiko|ja|島津珍彦}}
*{{ill|Kono Shrine|ja|籠神社}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:しいねつひこ}}
[[Category:日本の神 (人物神 記紀)]]
[[Category:日本神話の人物]]
[[Category:古代日本の人物]]
[[Category:倭氏]]
[[Category:国津神]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:椎根津彦]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
